---
title: Prerequisites
---

You **don't** need the `bp-submission-pipeline` to submit to BigPicture. If you
are comfortable on the command line, you can prepare and upload a dataset
yourself with the `sda-cli` direct path described in this section. Some of these
tools are also used inside the `bp-submission-pipeline`, so this path stays close
to what happens under the hood there.

::: {.callout-note}
This section assumes a more technical, command-line-comfortable reader than the
[Implementation (A–J)](../prerequisites.qmd) operator manual. If you'd rather run
an automated, guided flow, use that manual instead.
:::

This page installs the three tools the direct path needs. Use **at least** the
minimum version listed — older versions are missing commands this guide relies
on.

| Tool | Minimum version | Purpose |
|------|-----------------|---------|
| [`sda-cli`](https://github.com/NBISweden/sda-cli/releases/tag/v0.2.1) | v0.2.1 | Encrypt files and upload them to your BigPicture inbox |
| [`s3cmd`](https://github.com/s3tools/s3cmd/releases/tag/v2.2.0) | v2.2.0 | S3 transport used with the inbox configuration file |
| [`bigpicture-metadata-interface`](https://github.com/imi-bigpicture/bigpicture-metadata-interface/releases/tag/v2.0.2) | v2.0.2 | Generate and manage the dataset metadata XML *(private repository)* |

## sda-cli

Download the release that matches your operating system from the
[`sda-cli` releases page](https://github.com/NBISweden/sda-cli/releases/latest),
then extract the binary. The executable ends up in the same directory as the
downloaded archive.

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows

Download the `..._Windows_x86_64.zip` asset, then extract it:

```powershell
tar -xvzf sda-cli_vX_Windows_x86_64.zip
```

## macOS

Download the `..._Darwin_x86_64.tar.gz` asset, then extract it:

```bash
tar -xvzf sda-cli_vX_Darwin_x86_64.tar.gz
```

## Linux

Download the `..._Linux_x86_64.tar.gz` asset, then extract it:

```bash
tar -xvzf sda-cli_vX_Linux_x86_64.tar.gz
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

Run the binary's built-in help to confirm it executes:

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows
```powershell
sda-cli help
```
## macOS
```bash
./sda-cli help
```
## Linux
```bash
./sda-cli help
```
:::

You should see the usage text listing the `encrypt` and `upload` commands. Check
that you downloaded **v0.2.1 or later** from the releases page.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
Full `sda-cli` user documentation lives in its
[GitHub repository](https://github.com/NBISweden/sda-cli). This guide includes
everything needed to encrypt and upload to BigPicture.
:::

## s3cmd

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows

Install with `pip` (Python required):

```powershell
pip install "s3cmd>=2.2.0"
```

## macOS

```bash
brew install s3cmd
```

## Linux

```bash
sudo apt-get install -y s3cmd
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

```bash
s3cmd --version
```

Expected output (your patch version may differ):

```
s3cmd version 2.2.0
```
:::

## bigpicture-metadata-interface

This tool is distributed through a **private** Git repository, so you must have
been granted access before you can install it. Once you have access, install
**v2.0.2 or later** following the instructions in the repository's own README.

::: {.callout-important}
If you cannot reach the repository, you do not yet have access. Request it the
same way you request access to the other private BigPicture resources — see
[Support](../support.qmd).
:::

Once all three tools report a version, continue to [Prepare data](prepare-data.qmd).
